mechanism_narrative: A flat or falling FEDFUNDS rate keeps the discount rate anchor low, supporting equity multiples and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when CPI inflation is not accelerating, because real-rate pressure stays contained.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are negative or below baseline in periods with flat-to-declining FEDFUNDS and non-accelerating CPIAUCSL, or if the signal's rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in UNRATE above a recent low signals labor-market deterioration that eventually weakens earnings expectations and risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points from its 3-month low
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations show positive 60d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal loses directional accuracy in any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A strong monthly rise in RSAFS indicates stronger consumer demand and revenue momentum, which supports equity returns over the next 20 trading days through improved sales expectations and margin resilience.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth turns positive on a 3-month annualized basis
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS growth is followed by negative or below-median 20d SPX returns in a majority of triggered cases, or if the signal shows no stable positive IC over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO together with rising PAYEMS signals an expansion in real activity and hiring that improves cyclical earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising INDPRO and PAYEMS does not outperform either series alone for 60d SPX returns, or if the combined signal's effect disappears in rolling 3-year samples.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST below its recent level signals housing demand and cyclical growth weakening, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as investors price slower macro momentum.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% versus its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST declines are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in most triggered cases, or if the event-study average return is not materially below unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation is still rising, the market faces a tightening-plus-inflation squeeze that compresses valuation multiples and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL 3-month change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-rate and rising-inflation regime does not predict negative 60d SPX returns more often than not, or if the effect is indistinguishable from zero after controlling for each proxy separately.

mechanism_narrative: In a reopening or post-shock recovery regime, falling UNRATE combined with rising PAYEMS signals faster employment absorption, which supports equity returns over the next 5 trading days through higher growth expectations and lower recession risk.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor-improvement signal fails to produce positive 5d SPX returns in the recovery regime, or if the relationship reverses during the same regime across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, simultaneous slowing of GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates demand deceleration that should lead to weaker equity performance over the next 60 trading days as analysts cut revenue and profit forecasts.
failure_condition: Reject if synchronized slowing in GDPC1 and RSAFS does not precede negative 60d SPX returns more often than baseline, or if the joint signal loses sign stability across rolling 3-year periods.
